documentation.text: @notice Append-only list of every recorded attendance.
@notice Fast look-up: was this hash ever submitted?
@notice Index into `records` for a given hash (valid iff hashExists).

devdoc.author: AMS DApp — University of East London (CN6035)
devdoc.details: Each hash may be recorded at most once. Duplicates revert so the      contract doubles as an anti-replay guard for the check-in flow.

recordAttendance(bytes32).details: Reverts if the same hash has already been recorded.

params._attendanceHash: keccak256 digest produced by the backend.

returns.exists: True iff the hash has been recorded.
returns.student: Address that submitted the record (zero if absent).
returns.timestamp: Block timestamp of inclusion (zero if absent).

devdoc.title: AttendanceRecord

AttendanceRecorded(address,bytes32,uint2.notice: Emitted on every successful recordAttendance call.

getRecord(uint256).notice: Fetch a record by its index in insertion order.

getRecordCount().notice: Total number of records ever committed.

hashExists(bytes32).notice: Fast look-up: was this hash ever submitted?

hashToIndex(bytes32).notice: Index into `records` for a given hash (valid iff hashExists).

recordAttendance(bytes32).notice: Commits a new attendance hash to the ledger.

records(uint256).notice: Append-only list of every recorded attendance.

verifyAttendance(bytes32).notice: Look up a hash and return the originating address + timestamp.

userdoc.notice: Immutable ledger of attendance commitments. The backend hashes         (studentId, courseId, date) off-chain and submits only the digest,         so no PII is ever written to the chain.
